B2B Procurement Recommender System

Weichi Zhang · HSE Graduate School of Business · Master Thesis 2026

What This System Does

This recommender system analyzes real procurement data from Russia's MARKER platform to help institutional buyers make informed purchasing decisions.

1
Find Suppliers — Select your institution type and product to see which companies supply it, with prices and trust scores
2
Compare Prices — See how prices vary across Russian regions to know if you're paying fairly
3
Peer Analysis — Learn what products other institutions like yours commonly buy

Data Overview

Procurement contracts from state-owned institutions, sourced from Russia's MARKER platform (Interfax Group), July 2025 – January 2026.

Find Suppliers for Your Institution

Select your institution type, region, and product. The system shows which companies supply it to institutions like yours.

Compare Prices Across Regions

Select a product to see how prices vary across different Russian regions.

Price by Region

Price by Category

All product categories sorted by purchase volume. "Typical price" is the middle value — half of purchases were above it, half below.

What Do Institutions Like Yours Buy?

See what products are commonly purchased by the same type of institution. Region is optional.

Exploratory Data Analysis

Statistical analysis of the procurement dataset used to train the Two-Tower Neural Network.

Top 15 Products by Purchase Volume

Number of purchase records per product category, with buyer and supplier counts.

Purchases by Institution Type

Distribution of procurement activity across institution categories.

Top 20 Regions by Purchase Volume

Geographic distribution of procurement activity.

Monthly Purchase Trends

Temporal distribution showing the Q4 procurement cycle characteristic of Russian institutional budgeting.

Procurement Method Distribution

Tender types used, governed by Federal Law 44-FZ.

Buyer Interaction Density

Number of distinct product categories purchased per buyer. Shows the cold-start challenge: most buyers purchase very few categories.

Product Group Concentration

Market share by OKPD2 product group. Dairy dominance (68% of all buyer-product interactions) explains why the popularity baseline performs strongly.

Price Distribution Summary

Overall price statistics across all product categories (RUB per unit).

Model Evaluation Results

The Two-Tower Neural Network compared against two baselines using standard recommender system metrics.

Overall Performance (All Buyers)

ModelRecall@5Recall@10Recall@20NDCG@10
The Popularity Baseline scores high because dairy products account for 68% of all buyer-product interactions. When the majority of purchases are the same products, recommending "most popular" works artificially well.

Cold-Start Performance (Buyers with < 5 purchases)

ModelBuyersRecall@5Recall@10NDCG@10
The Two-Tower model achieves 35.3% Recall@5 for cold-start buyers by leveraging institutional features (type, region) rather than purchase history. This is the core value: useful recommendations for new institutions.

What Do These Metrics Mean?

Recall@K
Did the correct product appear in the model's top K recommendations? Higher = better.
NDCG@K
Was the correct product ranked near the top of the list, not buried at the bottom? Higher = better.
Cold-Start
Performance for institutions with very few (<5) purchase records. Tests if the model helps new buyers.
